title: सरस्वती द्वादश नाम स्तोत्रम्
devanagariText:
॥ श्री सरस्वती द्वादश नाम स्तोत्रम् ॥

सरस्वती महाभागा विद्या कमललोचना।
विश्वरूपा विशालाक्षी विद्याम्बा वेदनुतिनी॥१॥

भद्रकाली गोपिनीति शारदा बहुरूपिणी।
द्वादशैतानि नामानि सरस्वत्याः प्रकीर्तयेत्॥२॥

प्रथमं सरस्वती नाम द्वितीयं महाभागा।
तृतीयं विद्या प्रोक्तं चतुर्थं कमललोचना॥३॥

पञ्चमं विश्वरूपा च षष्ठं विशालाक्षी तथा।
सप्तमं विद्याम्बा वै अष्टमं वेदनुतिनी॥४॥

नवमं भद्रकाली च दशमं गोपिनी परा।
एकादशं शारदा च द्वादशं बहुरूपिणी॥५॥

द्वादशैतानि नामानि त्रिसन्ध्यं यः पठेन्नरः।
सर्वविद्याविनीतस्तु सर्वलोके च पूजितः॥६॥

जिह्वाग्रे वसते नित्यं सरस्वती सुभाषिणी।
द्वादशनामपाठेन कविर्भवति निश्चितम्॥७॥

॥ इति श्री सरस्वती द्वादश नाम स्तोत्रम् सम्पूर्णम् ॥
transliteration:
|| Shri Saraswati Dwadash Nama Stotram ||

Saraswati Mahabhaga Vidya Kamala-lochana.
Vishwarupa Vishalakshi Vidyamba Vedanutini.||1||

Bhadrakali Gopini-iti Sharada Bahurpini.
Dwadashaitani namani Saraswatyah prakirtayet.||2||

Prathamam Saraswati nama dwitiyam Mahabhaga.
Tritiyam Vidya proktam chaturtham Kamala-lochana.||3||

Panchamam Vishwarupa cha shashtham Vishalakshi tatha.
Saptamam Vidyamba vai ashtamam Vedanutini.||4||

Navamam Bhadrakali cha dashamam Gopini para.
Ekadasham Sharada cha dwadasham Bahurpini.||5||

Dwadashaitani namani tri-sandhyam yah pathen-narah.
Sarva-vidya-vinitastu sarvaloke cha pujitah.||6||

Jihvagre vasate nityam Saraswati subhashini.
Dwadasha-nama-pathena kavir-bhavati nishchitam.||7||

|| Iti Shri Saraswati Dwadash Nama Stotram Sampurnam ||
hindiMeaning: सरस्वती, महाभागा, विद्या, कमललोचना, विश्वरूपा, विशालाक्षी, विद्याम्बा, वेदनुतिनी, भद्रकाली, गोपिनी, शारदा और बहुरूपिणी - ये सरस्वती देवी के बारह नाम हैं। जो मनुष्य तीनों सन्ध्याओं में इन द्वादश नामों का पाठ करता है, वह सर्वविद्या में पारंगत और सर्वलोक में पूजित होता है। इन नामों के पाठ से जिह्वा पर सरस्वती का निवास होता है और व्यक्ति निश्चित रूप से कवि बनता है।
benefits: Twelve sacred names of Goddess Saraswati
Invokes mastery in all forms of knowledge (vidya)
Makes one respected and honored in all worlds
Saraswati resides on the tongue of the reciter
Invokes the blessing of poetic abilities and eloquence
description: Dedicated to Goddess Saraswati, the Saraswati Dwadash Nama Stotram (सरस्वती द्वादश नाम स्तोत्रम्) is a devotional hymn of praise revered in Hindu tradition. This sacred composition is sourced from Traditional and continues to inspire devotees worldwide. Regular recitation is believed to twelve sacred names of Goddess Saraswati, grant mastery in all forms of knowledge (vidya), and saraswati resides on the tongue of the reciter. Recitation is particularly auspicious on Wednesday (Budhvar) and Thursday (Guruvar) and during Vasant Panchami. Comprising 7 verses, it can be completed in approximately 5 minutes, making it suitable for daily devotional practice. On this page, you can read the complete Saraswati Dwadash Nama Stotram in Devanagari Sanskrit with English transliteration, Hindi meaning (arth), and free PDF download for offline recitation.
seoDescription: Read Saraswati Dwadash Nama - 12 names of Goddess Saraswati in Sanskrit with Hindi meaning. Chant for wisdom, knowledge and poetic ability.
relatedSearches: saraswati dwadash nama stotra in hindi

seoFaqs.question: What are the 12 names of Saraswati in the Dwadash Nama Stotra?
seoFaqs.answer: The twelve names are: Bharati, Saraswati, Sharada, Hamsavahini, Jagadvikhyata, Vageshwari, Kaumari, Brahmacharini, Buddhidatri, Varadayini, Kshudraghanta, and Bhuvaneshwari. Each name reflects an aspect of the goddess's role as patron of knowledge, speech, and creative power.

seoFaqs.question: What are the benefits of chanting Saraswati Dwadash Nama Stotra?
seoFaqs.answer: The phalashruti states that one who recites the twelve names of Saraswati at dawn, noon, and dusk receives the goddess's full blessings — sharp intellect, eloquence, command over the arts, success in studies, and freedom from ignorance.

seoFaqs.question: When should Saraswati Dwadash Nama be chanted?
seoFaqs.answer: Traditional practice prescribes recitation at the three sandhyas (dawn, noon, dusk), especially on Vasant Panchami (Saraswati's principal festival), on Wednesdays (the day of Budha, lord of intellect), during Navaratri's Saraswati puja days, and before examinations or any intellectual undertaking.

seoFaqs.question: Who is Goddess Saraswati?
seoFaqs.answer: Saraswati is the consort of Brahma and the presiding deity of knowledge, music, arts, language, and wisdom. She is depicted seated on a white lotus or swan (hamsa), holding a veena, a book, and a mala — symbols of music, learning, and meditation.

seoFaqs.question: What is the meaning of Hamsavahini?
seoFaqs.answer: Hamsavahini means 'she who rides the swan'. The hamsa (swan) symbolizes viveka — the discriminative wisdom that separates truth from illusion (like the legendary swan that separates milk from water). It represents Saraswati's role as the granter of pure discernment.
